title: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) managed-cluster configuration
description: Per-AKS-managed-cluster hardening posture captured read-only from Azure Resource Manager (ARM Reader role): Kubernetes RBAC enabled, network-policy plugin, private-cluster / authorized-IP-range API-server isolation, managed-identity vs service-principal, local-accounts-disabled, OIDC issuer, node-pool count, and Kubernetes version. Management-plane CLUSTER configuration only — NEVER admin kubeconfig / cluster-admin credentials (listClusterAdminCredential is not called), service-principal secrets, workload/pod manifests, container images, or container contents. The platform evaluator decides pass/fail per (control, cluster); the connector emits a descriptive default verdict. x-default-scf-anchors are default mapping hints flagged for maintainer recheck (OQ #9).

cluster_id.description: ARM resource id of the AKS managed cluster.

subscription_id.description: Subscription the managed cluster lives in.

resource_group.description: Resource group containing the managed cluster.

location.description: Azure region of the managed cluster.

kubernetes_version.description: Kubernetes control-plane version the cluster runs.

rbac_enabled.description: Kubernetes RBAC is enabled on the cluster.

network_policy.description: Network-policy plugin configured (e.g. calico | azure); empty means none.

private_cluster.description: API server is provisioned as a private cluster (no public API endpoint).

authorized_ip_ranges.description: API-server access is restricted to authorized IP ranges.

managed_identity.description: Cluster uses a managed identity rather than a service principal.

local_accounts_disabled.description: Local (static cluster-admin) accounts are disabled on the cluster.

oidc_issuer_enabled.description: The cluster's OIDC issuer (workload-identity federation) is enabled.

node_pool_count.description: Number of agent (node) pools configured on the cluster.
